content:
LLMs are now being used to control robots by generating executable code from natural language.  
CaP-X is a new benchmark that evaluates this capability on real robotic manipulation tasks.  
Developed by researchers from top institutions, it highlights both progress and current limitations.

• Models generate control code directly from language, enabling robots to perform physical tasks  
• Top models achieve ~30% success, far below human performance of ~90%  
• Performance improves significantly with reinforcement learning and transfers well to real robots  
• Higher-level task definitions boost performance, suggesting future systems will combine reasoning with specialized control
